title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs postgresql 18 domain schema, keys, constraints, and index intent from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for constrained relational feed-service schema after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review Go team handles case 3904 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.

The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 3739 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Include tenant ownership in keys, constraints, queries, tests, and audit evidence so cross-tenant references cannot form.
